title: Topology Optimization (SIMP)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Topology Optimization (SIMP): SIMP topology optimization: minimize structural compliance subject to volume fraction constraint using density-based penalization. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG (F.filter.density…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~200; load_direction_error, boundary_condition_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: K(rho)*u = f, C = f ** T u, rho_e in [rho_min, 1], penalty p=3
E.world_state_x: density field rho in [0,1]^N_e, N_e finite elements
E.observation_y: compliance C = f ** T K(rho)^{-1} f (scalar objective)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D_density_field is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 200); load_direction_error dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Deterministic sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.solver_class: gradient-based [optimality_criteria or MMA (Method of Moving Asymptotes)]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: C_opt / C_init <= epsilon after N_iter iterations. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~200. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |load_direction_error|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_e * N_dof) per iteration (FEM solve + sensitivity)
